summary: Proprietary data layer testing completed successfully - Competitive Moat Secured! All 9 new proprietary endpoints tested and passed. Verified AI Tools Database (15 enterprise tools including GPT-5.2, Claude, UiPath, Textract), Task-to-Tool Recommendations with fit scores and architectures, Implementation Complexity with real-world costs and success rates, Task Decomposition with atomic subtasks, Failure Mode Analysis with probabilities and mitigation, ROI Calculations with custom payback analysis, Automation Blueprints combining all data, and Salary Benchmarks across regions.
passed_tests: AI Tools Database - 15 enterprise tools (GPT-5.2, Claude Sonnet 4.5, UiPath Enterprise, AWS Textract, etc.) with vendor details, pricing models, and enterprise compliance flags
Tools Recommendations - Task-to-tool mapping with fit scores, use cases, setup hours, and recommended architectures (e.g., 'Textract for OCR -> UiPath for data entry -> Human review')
Automation Blueprint (task-005) - Complete implementation plan combining tools, complexity, risk analysis, and business case in single endpoint
Quick Wins endpoint - High-ROI opportunities (167% Year 1 ROI, 4.5 month payback for data entry automation)
Custom ROI Calculator - Accepts salary, automation %, costs and returns detailed business case with payback analysis
Salary Benchmarks - Multi-region (USA/Canada/EU) with fully-loaded cost multipliers for accurate ROI calculations
Task Decomposition - Atomic subtask breakdown with automation potential and time savings analysis
Implementation Complexity - Real-world difficulty scores with costs, timelines, required roles, and industry success rates
Failure Mode Analysis - What breaks, probabilities, detection methods, mitigation strategies, and recommended SLAs
Database status verification - All proprietary data properly loaded and accessible
Root endpoint documentation - API structure and version information correct
success_percentage: backend: 100% (15/15 proprietary endpoint tests passed), frontend: N/A (backend-only testing requested)
